_protocol: Calibration topic — prospective. Fully populated 2026-04-11. First posterior update requires fresh intel search.

meta.title: South China Sea Kinetic Incident by End 2026
meta.question: Will a kinetic military incident with casualties occur between PRC forces and any claimant state in the South China Sea by December 31, 2026?
meta.resolution: Confirmed shots fired at military/coast guard vessel, ramming causing documented injury or death, boarding of military vessel resulting in injury/death, or water cannon causing documented serious injury. Must be reported by at least two independent primary sources (wire service, government statement, or verified video evidence). Commercial vessel incidents alone do not resolve.

tagConfig._docs: MILITARY = PLA/PCG/AFP actions verified by imagery or official statement. EVENT = incident confirmed. RHETORIC = statements, warnings, demands. DIPLOMATIC = talks, frameworks, backchannel signals. DATA = quantitative feed updates. INTEL = unverified or single-source claim.

H1.label: Gray zone only — no casualties through Dec 31, 2026
H1.description: PRC continues harassment operations (water cannon, laser, close intercept, blocking maneuvers) but no confirmed casualties. Philippines continues resupply missions under pressure. Pattern persists without crossing kinetic threshold. Dominant historical outcome; reinforced by PH-China talks resuming and ADIZ incursions declining.

H2.label: Kinetic incident with injuries but no deaths
H2.description: At least one Philippine (or other claimant) sailor/guardsman sustains documented injury from PRC action (water cannon trauma, ramming injuries, physical altercation during boarding attempt). No fatalities. Most plausible escalation path given current trajectory: water cannon incidents have already come close, and proximity incidents like BRP Benguet / Jingzhou (March 30) raise collision risk.

H3.label: Lethal incident — at least one fatality
H3.description: A kinetic interaction results in at least one confirmed fatality on either side. Could result from: deliberate live fire, severe ramming, helicopter downing, or a water cannon incident causing drowning. PRC has historically avoided lethal outcomes due to MDT tripwire awareness, but escalation dynamics and miscalculation risk are non-trivial over an 8-month horizon.

H4.label: MDT Article IV invocation by Philippines
H4.description: Philippines formally invokes the 1951 Mutual Defense Treaty Article IV following a PRC attack on Philippine armed forces or public vessels. Requires: (1) lethal or severe kinetic incident, (2) Marcos administration political will to invoke, (3) US willingness to respond. Historical base rate: never invoked since treaty signed. Conditional on H3 occurring, probability of invocation estimated at ~50%, implying ~0.065 unconditional.

posteriorHistory.note: Initial priors set 2026-04-11. Informed by: no lethal SCS military incident in modern era, current BRP Benguet near-miss, water cannon escalation trend, active PH-China talks, ADIZ incursions declining below 200/month. 8-month horizon (~245 days).
Escalation but strictly gray-zone — environmental sabotage + physical barricade without injuries strengthens H1 path; does not advance toward kinetic/lethal thresholds.

redTeam.challenge: No significant shifts proposed; no challenge needed.

escalation_ladder._docs: Tracks position on the SCS gray-to-kinetic escalation ladder. Each rung up increases H2/H3/H4 probability.

rung_labels.1: Verbal warnings and navigation challenges only
rung_labels.4: Ramming or physical contact, near-miss weapons fire

escalation_ladder.note: Set to rung 3 based on confirmed water cannon incidents and BRP Benguet proximity incident Mar 30 2026.

ph_china_diplomatic_track._docs: State of the bilateral diplomatic channel. Active talks suppress escalation; breakdown accelerates it.
ph_china_diplomatic_track.description: High-level talks resumed Q1 2026 after suspension. PH-China back-channel reportedly active. China defense ministry issued 'stop provocations' statement April 2026 — read as rhetorical pressure, not diplomatic progress.

us_commitment_signal._docs: US posture signals to both PH and PRC. Stronger signals deter PRC escalation but may embolden PH assertiveness.
us_commitment_signal.recent_signal: USS Carl Vinson CSG in South China Sea Q1 2026
us_commitment_signal.note: High US commitment level both deters PRC escalation and raises stakes of any incident. Net effect: moderate deterrence.

_design_rules: All indicators must be OBSERVABLE ACTIONS or VERIFIABLE DATA — not rhetoric or stated intentions
Threshold for 'fired' must be specific enough that two analysts would agree
Hypotheses H2-H4 are nested: H3 implies H2 occurred or was bypassed; H4 implies H3
Anti-indicators for H1 are pro-indicators for H2/H3 and vice versa

tier1_critical.desc: PLA Navy or PCG vessel fires live ammunition (warning shots or directed fire) at Philippine military, coast guard, or resupply vessel. Must be confirmed by AFP statement, video evidence, or two independent wire reports.
Philippine (or other claimant state) military or coast guard personnel confirmed killed or seriously injured in incident with PRC forces. AFP official statement or hospital documentation required.
tier1_critical.posteriorEffect: H1 -40pp, H2 -10pp, H3 +35pp, H4 +15pp. Resolves H2 at minimum.

tier1_critical.desc: Philippines formally notifies US of Article IV invocation following armed attack. Requires official Malacanang or DFA statement invoking the 1951 MDT.
Deliberate ramming of Philippine Navy vessel by PLA Navy warship (not coast guard nudge — warship-to-warship collision with structural damage). Confirmed by AFP imagery or satellite.
tier1_critical.posteriorEffect: H1 -25pp, H2 +15pp, H3 +10pp. Near-certain precursor to H3.

tier2_strong.desc: PCG or PLA Navy water cannon use results in documented injury to Philippine personnel. Requires AFP medical record acknowledgment or official statement citing injuries.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H1 -15pp, H2 +12pp, H3 +3pp. Partial resolution signal for H2.

tier2_strong.desc: PLA Navy or PCG personnel attempt to physically board Philippine vessel (not just shadow/intercept). Must be confirmed by video or AFP statement.
PLA Navy warship (not PCG) closes to within 100m of Philippine Navy vessel in contested waters, confirmed by AFP imagery or OSINT satellite. Escalation beyond current pattern.
tier2_strong.note: BRP Benguet near-miss with frigate Jingzhou on March 30, 2026. Distance not officially confirmed but characterized as 'near-miss.'

tier2_strong.desc: ADIZ incursions return to above 300/month for two consecutive months, reversing current downtrend. Suggests PRC strategic shift to pressure rather than calibrated restraint.
Philippines formally suspends or withdraws from bilateral SCS talks with China, citing PRC provocation. Official Malacanang or DFA announcement required.
US Navy warship formally escorts Philippine resupply mission to Second Thomas Shoal or other contested feature. Official INDOPACOM statement or confirmed by both AFP and USN.
tier2_strong.note: Would dramatically raise stakes of any PRC intercept attempt.
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: H1 +5pp (deters PRC), H4 +5pp (raises invocation likelihood if incident still occurs)
tier2_strong.lr_migration_warning: design prior drift >30% — review before next firing

tier3_suggestive.desc: ADIZ incursions sustain below 150/month for 3+ consecutive months. Suggests PRC strategic restraint.

PRC makes observable diplomatic gesture: withdraws vessel from contested feature, resumes fisheries agreement talks, or allows uncontested PH resupply mission. Observable action required, not just statement.
Philippines increases frequency of resupply missions to Second Thomas Shoal beyond current cadence (publicly stated), OR deploys additional BRP vessels to contested features.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H1 -3pp, H2 +2pp, H3 +1pp. Increases contact frequency.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Satellite imagery confirms new PRC construction or facility expansion on contested SCS feature (not already under construction as of April 2026).
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: H1 -2pp, H2 +1pp, H3 +1pp. Signals strategic entrenchment.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Balikatan 2026 exercises complete as planned with 500+ events confirmed. Signals sustained US commitment without direct escalation.
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: Minor: H1 +2pp (deters), H4 +1pp (strengthens invocation pathway if incident occurs)

tier3_suggestive.desc: Xi Jinping faces measurable domestic criticism (official media, CPPCC, NPC) over SCS policy or economic headwinds creating incentive to manufacture nationalist rally event. Hard to detect; flag if credible OSINT emerges.

anti_indicators.desc: PRC and Philippines establish a functioning military-to-military hotline with documented first use during an incident. Reduces likelihood of miscalculation escalating to injuries.
PRC withdraws PCG or PLAN vessels from immediate vicinity of Second Thomas Shoal or Scarborough Shoal for 30+ consecutive days. Observable via AIS and satellite.

Marcos administration explicitly states it will NOT invoke MDT even if lethal incident occurs (hedging statement). Or: Philippine Congress passes resolution limiting president's MDT invocation authority.
anti_indicators.note: PH executive currently maintains ambiguity. An explicit de-escalatory statement would reduce H4 materially.
anti_indicators.lr_migration_warning: design prior drift >30% — review before next firing

anti_indicators.desc: Three or more distinct water cannon or physical intercept incidents within any 30-day window, indicating normalized escalation above current baseline.

actorModel.description: Five key decision-makers shape the escalation path. PRC controls the primary levers; Philippines the response; US the deterrence architecture; Xi and Marcos hold political override authority.

pla_navy.name: PLA Navy / Southern Theater Command
pla_navy.role: Primary aggressor / enforcer of PRC territorial claims. Operates PLAN warships in SCS; coordinates with PCG.
pla_navy.decisionStyle: Institutional, hierarchical. Follows Standing Orders from CMC/Xi. Theater command has some tactical discretion on intercept geometry and intensity.
biases: Escalation dominance preference — defaults to more pressure rather than less when Philippines resists
Face/prestige calculation: backing down from a confrontation is institutionally costly
MDT tripwire awareness: lethal incident involving AFP triggers US treaty obligation; strong incentive to stay below that threshold
filters: CMC approval likely required for live fire at foreign military vessel
PCG (not PLAN) is preferred instrument for gray zone ops to maintain deniability
Operations orders likely specify maximum aggression level per incident type
overrides: Xi personal directive in response to perceived Philippine provocation
Accidental escalation during close-proximity maneuver (BRP Benguet precedent)
Junior officer miscalculation during night or low-visibility operation

pcg_china_coast_guard.name: China Coast Guard (PCG)
pcg_china_coast_guard.role: Primary instrument for gray zone harassment. Operates water cannon, blocks resupply, enforces exclusion zones. Deniability buffer for PLAN.
pcg_china_coast_guard.decisionStyle: Operational-level tactical decisions; follows PLAN/CMC strategic direction. More willing to use physical contact than PLAN because non-military framing.
biases: Water cannon and physical interception are normalized tactics — low threshold for use
Views Philippine resupply missions as provocations justifying response
filters: Live fire would require CMC approval
Cannot formally board foreign military vessel without creating treaty incident
overrides: Escalating orders from Southern Theater following PH 'provocation'

philippine_navy_afp.name: Philippine Navy / Armed Forces of the Philippines
philippine_navy_afp.role: Defender of Philippine territorial claims. Conducts resupply missions to BRP Sierra Madre at Second Thomas Shoal. Primary target of PRC harassment.
philippine_navy_afp.decisionStyle: Institutional, reactive. Follows Malacanang political guidance on how assertively to pursue resupply missions. Under Marcos Jr., posture has been more public (media transparency) but not more kinetically assertive.
biases: Transparency tactic (bringing journalists) creates documentation but also escalation pressure
MDT reliance: AFP assumes US will respond to lethal incident; may accept higher risk accordingly
Institutional pride: surrendering access to Sierra Madre is seen as strategic defeat
filters: Rules of engagement likely prohibit first use of weapons against PRC
Marcos political direction is the binding constraint on assertiveness level
overrides: Lethal PRC action against AFP personnel would trigger AFP demand for MDT invocation
Strong domestic political pressure following visible humiliation (e.g., seized vessel)

xi_jinping.name: Xi Jinping
xi_jinping.role: Ultimate decision authority on PRC SCS policy. CMC Chair. Controls the political override on escalation ceiling.
xi_jinping.decisionStyle: Strategic patience combined with tactical assertiveness. Tolerates high-tempo gray zone ops but historically avoids actions that trigger US military response. Responds to perceived weakness with escalation, perceived strength with recalibration.
biases: Taiwan priority: SCS incidents that trigger US intervention distract from Taiwan scenario; incentive to keep SCS below MDT threshold
Nationalist legitimacy: appearing weak on South China Sea claims is domestically costly
Economic headwinds in 2026 may increase or decrease appetite for foreign distraction
filters: US MDT tripwire is the primary filter against lethal incidents
ASEAN diplomatic relationships constrain overt military aggression against Southeast Asian claimants
overrides: Perceived US weakness or distraction (e.g., Middle East focus) could lower Xi's threshold
Major Philippine provocation (e.g., permanent USMC presence on Sierra Madre) might trigger stronger response

marcos_jr.name: Ferdinand Marcos Jr.
marcos_jr.role: Philippine president. Political decision authority on MDT invocation, assertiveness level of resupply missions, and public framing of incidents.
marcos_jr.decisionStyle: Pragmatic nationalist. Has moved Philippines toward stronger US alignment vs. Duterte era, but maintains economic ties with China. Transparency strategy (media on missions) suggests preference for international pressure over direct military confrontation.
biases: Domestic political pressure to not appear weak on territorial claims
Economic dependency on China trade creates incentive to avoid full rupture
US alliance provides strategic umbrella but Marcos has been careful not to over-rely
filters: Article IV invocation requires high political threshold — lethal incident plus clear attribution
Preference for international legal/media pressure over military escalation
overrides: Killing of Philippine sailors would likely force invocation regardless of diplomatic preferences
Domestic political crisis or election pressure could raise or lower assertiveness

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC — only verified events move posteriors. China defense ministry statements do not.
TAG EVERYTHING — separate PCG harassment (EVENT/MILITARY) from diplomatic statements (RHETORIC/DIPLOMATIC)
DON'T FRONT-RUN — BRP Benguet near-miss is t2_warship_proximity_under_100m; do not pre-score t1_casualty_confirmed
ACCIDENTAL ESCALATION RISK — miscalculation is a realistic pathway; watch for night/weather/high-speed scenarios
MDT CONDITIONALITY — H4 is conditional on H3; do not score H4 without H3 evidence first

evidenceLog.text: Philippines confirmed Chinese boats used cyanide at Second Thomas Shoal (Ayungin Shoal), calling it sabotage intended to kill local fish populations and deprive Navy personnel of a food source. Lab tests confirmed cyanide in seized bottles. Philippine NSC Spokesperson Cornelio Valencia warned the substance could weaken the reef supporting the grounded warship BRP Sierra Madre.
evidenceLog.posteriorImpact: NONE. H1-directional (gray zone intensification without casualties). No indicator fired. Cyanide is novel gray-zone tactic but does not meet any tier 1/2 threshold (no live fire, no casualties, no boarding attempt).
evidenceLog.note: Novel escalation vector: environmental/chemical sabotage at Second Thomas Shoal. Watch for PH response - could accelerate t2_ph_china_talks_collapse.

BLUE.reasoning: Sustained resupply missions reflect diplomatic-military status quo management; an absorption equilibrium in gray zone.

RED.reasoning: Eight resupply missions assumes the gray-zone posture holds. RED: PLA-N or CCG could interdict one and force a cascade. Suggest adding language about incident risk during the resupplies.

VIOLET.reasoning: Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF gray zone only through Dec 2026, THEN cumulative incidents (minor collisions, water cannons, boarding attempts) occur >=15 times but zero result in casualties or deaths
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Observable: OSINT/official logs document >=15 gray-zone incidents (collision, water cannon, attempted boarding) with confirmed zero casualties through 2026-12-31

AMBER.reasoning: Gray-zone incident counting with zero-casualty condition is well-specified. 0.75 plausible.

BLUE.reasoning: Gray-zone incidents without casualties IS the BLUE outcome: institutional management of tension, de-escalation through signaling, no kinetic escalation. 0.75 reasonable — possibly even low.

RED.reasoning: 15 gray-zone incidents with ZERO casualties through 2026 is naively optimistic. RED: escalation ladder has steep cliff; water-cannon incidents cause injuries routinely. Historical base rate suggests casualties within 18 months of >10 incidents. 0.75 probability dangerously understates escalation risk.

VIOLET.reasoning: Casualty threshold depends on Xi's China Coast Guard rules-of-engagement decisions and Marcos' AFP response protocols. Xi faces real incentive to keep gray-zone below casualty threshold (avoid triggering MDT with US under Trump). Probability is reasonable but driven by named decision-makers' restraint, not structural.

GRAY.reasoning: Compound count + zero-casualty threshold is a strong falsifier.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H1 (gray zone continues, no casualties through EOY), THEN institutional cost-shifting mirrors Thucydidean dynamic: US/allied forward-deployment increases 20-30% in tonnage/capex through 2026 in response to perceived escalation risk; China mirrors response symmetrically. conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Observable: Track DoD budget allocation for Pacific Fleet/7th Fleet & US Indo-Pacific Command through FY2027 vs FY2025 baseline. ≥20pp real increase in tonnage/capex deployed within theater. AMBER.reasoning: Security-dilemma lock-in is a canonical regime-hardening dynamic. Well-specified.

BLUE.reasoning: 20-30pp real capex increase in two years is extreme. DoD budget growth historically slower; appropriations cycles lag. BLUE sees diplomatic off-ramps (crisis-management hotlines, incident protocols) moderating arms race. 0.64 too high.

RED.reasoning: Security dilemma/arms race lock-in is core RED dynamic.

VIOLET.reasoning: Trump's named posture on alliances is NOT symmetric escalation — he's demanded allies pay more and has questioned forward deployment. DoD budget through FY2027 will reflect Hegseth's priorities and OMB (Vought) directives, not automatic arms-race dynamic. 20-30pp increase assumes structural response that named actors may reject.
VIOLET.modified_suggestion: Lower to 0.45; Trump/Hegseth/Vought named preferences may REDUCE forward deployment, not increase it.

GRAY.reasoning: DoD budget + PLA OSINT data both observable; arms-race lock-in thesis is well-specified.
